question: How does Loom's feature permissions model work?
expectedAnswer: A Fabric-style model: capabilities (editor.notebook, admin.tenant-settings, workload.warehouse) grouped into domains and workloads, granted to Entra users or groups with Reader, Contributor, or Admin roles. Grants are tuples stored in Cosmos and managed at /admin/permissions.

question: Does granting a workload capability cover its child editors?
expectedAnswer: Yes — grants on a parent capability propagate to every child; granting workload.warehouse covers every warehouse editor automatically, because enforcement walks the capability's ancestor chain.

question: How do bootstrap admins get access before any grants exist?
expectedAnswer: Two env vars set in admin-plane bicep: LOOM_TENANT_ADMIN_OID (a single user oid) and LOOM_TENANT_ADMIN_GROUP_ID (comma-separated group oids) bypass all permission checks so tenant admins can grant access from the empty state.

question: How is a permission check enforced on a BFF route?
expectedAnswer: Every route calls enforceCapability(session, capabilityId, role): 401 with no session, resolves the caller's oid + group oids, walks the ancestor chain, runs one Cosmos point query, and returns a structured 403 with an actionable remediation that the frontend renders in a warning MessageBar.

question: What does the grant dialog's Entra principal search require?
expectedAnswer: The BFF uses the Console UAMI's Microsoft Graph token, so the UAMI must hold admin-consented User.Read.All and Group.Read.All application permissions.

question: How do access review campaigns work?
expectedAnswer: The /admin/access-reviews builder wizard scopes a campaign (package/resource/principal/group/all) and snapshots ledger grants into the access-reviews store; reviewers attest or revoke per item (with bulk actions and delegation), and closing a campaign with autoRevokeOnExpiry really revokes undecided assignments.

question: What happens to undecided items when a review expires?
expectedAnswer: With autoRevokeOnExpiry, close auto-revokes undecided items via a real revokeAssignment; an hourly sweeper (plus an admin Run review sweep action) closes past-deadline campaigns.

question: Are recurring access reviews supported?
expectedAnswer: Yes — a cadence picker (30/60/90/180/365 days) with a due date; nextDueDate drives the recurring cycle and the review sweep closes overdue campaigns.

question: What is the graph-group-sync gate and what works without it?
expectedAnswer: graph-group-sync is the sole day-one gate: opt-in via LOOM_GRAPH_GROUP_SYNC_ENABLED=true plus Graph Group.Read.All and GroupMember.Read.All, registered in the gate registry and on /admin/gates. Without it, group-targeted packages are still requestable directly — only the automatic membership reconcile is gated.

question: Can a user request access straight from a 403?
expectedAnswer: Yes — the shared RequestAccessInline component surfaces qualifying access packages for the blocked resource (GET /api/access-packages?resourceRef=) and submits the request from the gate itself.

question: How do I revoke everything a leaver has?
expectedAnswer: The admin Leaver revoke-all action calls POST /api/access-governance/revoke-all, revoking the principal's assignments in one step.

question: Where are feature-permission grants stored and what provisioning is needed?
expectedAnswer: In the Cosmos feature-permissions container (partition key /tenantId), auto-created by the BFF's createIfNotExists flow on first access — no bicep change required.
